forecaster_note: Tiny and flat to open the week — Tuesday is just windchop under clean offshores. The standout is WEDNESDAY: a solid dead-south groundswell (8.8ft @ 10s, 179°) fires ~1100kJ of energy (peaking near 1750), loading the south-exposed beaches — Long Reef well overhead, Curl Curl close behind — while Dee Why stays undersized as that dead-S angle wraps in poorly. A dawn session beats the midday southerly change. The weekend eases into fun 1–3ft ESE, a friendlier angle for the Point. — Surf Guru, read from Surfline model data (no written report scraped this run).

wind.summary: Light W offshore ~15 km/h, gusting 28 — clean faces, but there's no swell to groom today.

shark.summary: Active few days on the Northern Beaches — SLS drones cleared the water at three beaches in the last 24h: Long Reef (7:32am Mon 13th) and North Curl Curl (12:08pm Mon 13th) — both your southern spots — plus a fresh sighting at Palm Beach this morning (7:11am Tue 14th). All drone sightings, water evacuated each time. Stay switched on, especially at Long Reef and Curl Curl.

days.label: Today
days.note: Flat and poor across the board — only ~7kJ of energy and the biggest reading is 3-second NW windchop, so nothing rideable is reaching the banks. Clean light W offshore, but there's no swell to work with. A rest day (and it's a Tuesday anyway) — save your legs for Wednesday's south pulse. 🦈 Heads up — drones evacuated Long Reef (Mon 7:32am) and North Curl Curl (Mon 12:08pm), plus Palm Beach this morning. Both your southern options had sightings yesterday, so stay alert if the water's not as flat as forecast.

swells.label: NW windchop — short period, not rideable

dee-why.window: Nothing on offer — flat to knee-high dribble all day. Skip it.

tip.label: Rest day
tip.text: Only 7kJ and the dominant reading is 3-second NW chop — no organised swell reaching the Point. Nothing frontside to chase; save it for Wednesday's dead-S pulse.

swells.label: NW windchop — short period, not rideable

long-reef.window: The only spot with a chance of a rideable one — the S-facing reef milks the last scrap of S energy. Knee-high longboard glide at best.

tip.label: Spot call
tip.text: 7kJ at ~25/100 — Long Reef's S exposure squeezes out the odd knee-high runner. Floatiest board or a log if you're desperate for a paddle.

swells.label: NW windchop — short period, not rideable

curl-curl.window: Level with Long Reef — the southern corner banks bend in what little S there is. Small, clean, empty.

tip.label: Spot call
tip.text: Same 7kJ story as Long Reef. If you must surf, this or Long Reef are the only two holding anything — volume is your friend.

swells.label: NW windchop — short period, not rideable

manly.window: Most sheltered of the four — near-flat. Not worth the drive.

tip.label: Skip
tip.text: North-end shelter kills what little swell there is — shin-high and soft. Long Reef or Curl Curl over this today.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: The day of the week. A solid S groundswell fills in — 8.8ft @ 10s from a dead-south 179° — lifting energy from 7kJ today to ~1100kJ (peaking near 1750). Dawn SW winds are lightly offshore before the southerly change swings SSW/S cross-shore around midday, so it's an early or nothing. Because the swell is dead-S it wraps in poorly at Dee Why (2–3ft, chest to 1ft OH) while the open, S-exposed beaches load up — Long Reef 2–6ft and Curl Curl 2–5ft. Get out at first light.

dee-why.window: Dawn is the window — light SW offshore before the change swings cross around midday. Dead-S wrap keeps the Point smaller than the open beaches, but the rights should run early.

long-reef.window: Biggest of the four — the S-facing reef takes the dead-S swell head-on and could push well overhead on the sets. Dawn patrol before the wind ruins it; respect the size.

curl-curl.window: Punchy overhead beach-break peaks early — the performance option if you want open faces. Get in before the SSW change fills in mid-morning.

manly.window: The most forgiving pick if the swell overdelivers — north-end shelter trims the dead-S down to a manageable waist-to-shoulder.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: Easing back and swinging E. An ESE 3.8ft @ 6s tops up with a small ENE 1.5ft @ 8s, for 1–3ft waist–chest and ~160kJ. The ESE 123° angle sits far closer to your favoured NE–ENE window and wraps into Dee Why much better than Wednesday's dead-S — but winds are light S cross-shore, so it's fun-size rather than pumping. Saturday and Sunday look similar; pick whichever morning is glassiest.

dee-why.window: The ESE angle (123°) finally wraps into the Point where dead-S never does — go for the 10am window on whichever morning is cleaner. Frontside rights should be fun-size.

long-reef.window: Steady waist-to-shoulder peelers both days off the reef. Light S cross keeps it playful — mid-tide banks are the call.

curl-curl.window: Fun beach-break peaks — the punchiest option of the weekend if you want a bit of wall to hit.

manly.window: Rideable both days and the most protected from the S cross-shore — the safe pick if the wind gets up.
